criteria.label: Explain what a bundler does (resolve, transpile, tree-shake, minify, split).
criteria.example: Bundler stages: resolve (`import './x'` → file path) → transpile (TS/JSX → JS) → tree-shake (drop unused exports) → minify (rename, drop whitespace) → split (separate vendor/route chunks). One artefact tree the browser can load efficiently.

criteria.label: Use Vite scripts for dev and production builds.
Separate type-checking (tsc) from fast transpilation.
criteria.example:
```json
"scripts": {
  "build": "tsc --noEmit && vite build",
  "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit"
}
```
Vite/esbuild strip types fast; `tsc` checks them — both jobs, neither blocking the other in dev.

criteria.label: Write tree-shakeable imports and avoid bundling secrets into the client.
criteria.example:
```ts
import { capitalize } from 'lodash-es'      // ✓ named ESM import — shakeable
import _ from 'lodash'                       // ✗ default CJS — drags everything in
const PUBLIC_KEY = import.meta.env.VITE_PUBLIC   // ✓ public
const SECRET     = import.meta.env.VITE_SECRET   // ✗ visible to users
```
